description_long: This is a classical-style ceramic vase with a rounded body, a narrow neck, and two tall, ornate handles that curve upward and attach at the rim and shoulder. The body is decorated with intricate red and black painted motifs, including stylized waves, floral elements, and a central figure scene reminiscent of ancient Greek pottery. The neck and handles are finished in a deep black, contrasting with the cream-colored background of the main vessel. A Greek key meander pattern encircles the upper shoulder, adding to the decorative richness. The surface appears smooth and slightly glossy, with fine detailing in the painted designs. The vase is likely hollow and lightweight, suitable for display in a decorative or cultural context.
description: A classical ceramic vase with ornate black handles, cream body, and red-black painted Greek motifs, including a central figure and geometric patterns.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Greek key pattern, red wave motifs, and part of the central figure scene.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one black handle and the continuation of the painted motifs, with less detail visible on the main body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full central figure scene, both handles, and the intricate painted decorations.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other handle and a partial view of the geometric and wave motifs.
